title: SIM swap and mobile account takeover targeting all phone users: Criminals gaining control of mobile numbers via email compromise to intercept 2FA codes, reset passwords, access banking and government services. Exploiting data breaches and phishing. Joint ACMA-Scamwatch alert as of 7 April 2026.

Fake investment bond impersonation scam targeting middle-income savers: Criminals impersonating Bunnings and other major firms offering fake environmental bonds with 9% returns, investment ranges $50k-$250k, claiming government-backed protection. ASIC takedowns ongoing. Latest alert 19 May 2026.

Fake crypto trading platform pump-and-dump via encrypted messaging: Scammers infiltrating share trading and stock tips groups on WhatsApp/messaging apps, promoting fraudulent platforms showing fake trades and profits. All deposits stolen; fake asset release fees extracted. ASIC warning May 2026.

SMS/email refund and points expiry scam impersonating Qantas: Text and email phishing claiming prize redemption, point expiry or refund eligibility, directing to fake login pages. Qantas branding used. Ongoing since August 2025, resurgent in 2026.

Law enforcement impersonation crypto theft via false breach claims: Scammers filing fake ReportCyber reports using victims' identities, then calling claiming Operation Firestorm investigation involvement. Demand crypto transfers or seed phrase disclosure. AFP alert February 2026.

Fraudulent letters impersonating ASIC requesting account security transfers: Postal letters using ASIC branding falsely claiming bank staff under investigation, instructing recipients to transfer funds to secure accounts. ASIC never requests payments. CommBank alert April 2026.

Phone number spoofing impersonating ACCC requesting sensitive information: Scammers using spoofed ACCC reception numbers making unsolicited calls claiming to verify personal information, passwords, and bank details. Publicly available ACCC numbers spoofed. June 2025 alert, ongoing.

Seasonal worker loan fee scam targeting Pacific Australia Labour Mobility participants: Co-workers or community members offering assistance with personal loan applications for upfront fee, gaining banking access. Large unauthorized withdrawals follow loan approval. CommBank alert ongoing.

$9.4m in losses: Online shopping and parcel delivery scams exploiting holiday season urgency: Fake retail sites mimicking brands, missed delivery SMS directing to phishing sites, fraudulent sellers on marketplaces. 21,255 shopping reports, 5,690 delivery reports Jan-Oct 2025.

Hard-copy scratchie scams and fake lotteries via registered post: Physical scratch-off cards claiming prize prizes conditional on fee payment, delivered via registered mail. Financial losses increasing; exploits cost-of-living pressures with fake travel giveaway lures.

AI-generated voice impersonation of financial institutions and personal contacts emerging: NordVPN reports millions of scam domains detected Mar-Oct 2025. Deepfake phone calls impersonating banks and known individuals with highly realistic tone and familiarity. Unprecedented sophistication.

Age verification platform and government impersonation scams targeting Gen Z ahead of social media restrictions: Scammers impersonating age verification services requesting identity proof; others posing as law enforcement claiming breach of new 16+ laws, demanding fines. ANZ alert Dec 2025.
